persona.name: Katarina Novak

occupation.title: Deputy School Principal
occupation.organization: College Marcel Pagnol, Schiltigheim (a suburb of Strasbourg)
occupation.description: Manages the daily operations of a 600-student college (middle school), including timetable coordination, discipline, parent communication, and teacher support. Handles administrative crises — from student behavioral issues to facility problems. Reports to the principal and serves as acting head in his absence. High-stress role with emotional labor, especially when managing difficult family situations.

persona.education: CAPES in History-Geography (1996). Agregation interne in History (2008). Master's in Educational Sciences from Universite de Strasbourg (2005). Taught for 12 years before moving into administration. Born in Croatia — her parents emigrated to Alsace when she was 8.
long_term_goals: Become principal of her own establishment within 3-4 years
Redesign the apartment now that both children have moved out — create a proper reading room
Take up watercolor painting, which she abandoned when the children were small
Visit Croatia more regularly — reconnect with extended family in Split
persona.style: Katarina is poised, thorough, and quietly authoritative. She speaks in complete sentences with a slight Alsatian inflection and occasional Slavic rolling of Rs. She is the person who reads the entire document before commenting and asks the question no one else thought of. She dresses professionally but without flair — structured blouses, dark trousers, sensible shoes. At home she is warmer and more reflective, often with classical music playing. She is adapting to a quieter household and finding it both liberating and disorienting.

traits: Methodical and thorough — leaves nothing to chance in professional matters
Pragmatic about life transitions — approaches empty-nesting with resolve rather than sentiment
Intellectually curious but cautious about new technologies and services
Values competence and preparation; frustrated by carelessness in others
Dry humor that emerges in trusted company — students occasionally catch glimpses
Increasingly aware that she needs to invest in personal interests outside of work

interests: French and Croatian home cooking — her repertoire spans both traditions
Classical music and opera — subscriber to the Opera national du Rhin
Reading (historical fiction, biographies, Le Monde diplomatique)
Gardening on her apartment balcony — herbs, tomatoes, geraniums
Walking in the Foret de la Robertsau near Strasbourg
Exploring practical solutions for cooking-for-one or cooking-for-two
likes: Well-organized products with clear instructions and predictable outcomes
Meal solutions that help with the transition from cooking for a family to cooking for one or two
Quality ingredients — she knows the difference and is willing to pay for it
Flexible delivery schedules that accommodate her work travel
Services that feel respectful and adult — not trendy or youthful in their marketing
Seasonal menus that change with what is available locally
A quiet apartment, a good book, and a properly made cevapi
dislikes: Marketing aimed at young couples or families — she does not see herself in it
Subscription lock-in with auto-renewal and vague cancellation processes
Excessive packaging and portion sizes designed for larger households
Trendy superfoods and wellness marketing that lacks scientific grounding
Digital-first services that assume everyone is comfortable with apps
Being treated as a 'silver economy' target when she is 54, not 74
Food that is styled for Instagram rather than designed for taste

beliefs: Transitions in life require practical adaptation, not nostalgia
Cooking is a daily discipline that should bring satisfaction, not feel like a burden
Quality and simplicity are more valuable than variety for its own sake
Education — formal and self-directed — is the most reliable path to resilience
skills: Accomplished home cook across French and Croatian cuisines
Written communication and report drafting (French administrative style)

general: Shops twice weekly — Wednesday at the Schiltigheim market, Saturday at a Biocoop
Cooks every day but is adjusting recipes downward — finds it hard not to make family-sized portions
Has not tried a meal kit but her daughter suggested it; she is researching options cautiously
Reads product reviews in Le Monde and 60 Millions de Consommateurs before purchasing

morning: Up at 6:30, strong coffee with a slice of pain aux noix from the Alsatian bakery
Reviews the school day's schedule and any overnight parent emails
Walks 15 minutes to the college — she values the morning air
workday: At school by 7:45, handles administrative matters until classes begin
Morning: student discipline meetings, teacher consultations, scheduling
Lunch in the school canteen or a quick sandwich in her office
Afternoon: parent meetings, report writing, coordination with the principal
Leaves between 17:30 and 18:30 depending on the day
evening: Walks home, changes into comfortable clothes, turns on France Musique
Cooks dinner — often something simple now: a soup, a gratin, or a salad with roasted vegetables
Eats while reading or watching Arte — misses the family conversation at the table
Calls her husband or one of her children 3-4 evenings per week
weekend: Saturday: market shopping, deeper cooking project (sarma, choucroute, or a Croatian strudel), opera or concert in the evening
Sunday: walk in the Foret de la Robertsau, reading afternoon, meal planning for the week
Once a month visits her daughter in Lyon by TGV, or her son joins for Sunday lunch when he's free

health.physical: Good overall health for her age. Walks daily, no chronic conditions. Mild joint stiffness in the mornings. Had a routine mammogram scare two years ago that turned out benign — it made her more attentive to health screening. Watches portion sizes now that her activity level has decreased.
health.mental: Navigating the emotional adjustment of her husband's relocation (he works in Frankfurt during the week, home on weekends) and both children leaving. Not depressed but occasionally lonely on weekday evenings. Has considered joining a local association or choir for social connection.

relationships.name: Ivan Novak
relationships.description: Mechanical engineer at a German automotive supplier, currently posted to the Frankfurt office. Comes home to Strasbourg on weekends by ICE train. They have a solid marriage but the weekday separation is new and neither has fully adjusted. He is easygoing and supportive but not the most communicative by text.

relationships.name: Ana Novak
relationships.description: Junior architect at a firm in Lyon. Independent and opinionated — she suggested Katarina try a meal kit to avoid cooking 'depressing single portions.' Visits monthly and they have a warm, bantering relationship.

relationships.name: Marko Novak
relationships.description: Final-year engineering student at INSA Strasbourg. Lives in a student apartment across town. Comes for Sunday lunch when his schedule allows. More reserved than his sister but close to his mother.

relationships.name: Dragica Novak
relationships.description: Retired seamstress, lives in Strasbourg's Neudorf neighborhood. Born in Split, Croatia. Taught Katarina to cook Croatian dishes. They see each other weekly and Katarina helps with her groceries and appointments.
